political.s: Based on FEC data, Cava leans left with $0 raised in political donations and a 4000/15000 rep percentage (27% Republican). The company focuses on social justice initiatives and has made public commitments to supporting racial equity and Black communities.

charity.s: <cite index="30-12">Since 2022, Cava has contributed $1.2 million in food donations and financial contributions to local organizations.</cite> <cite index="25-16">In 2024, the company served over 42,000 meals to people in need through food bank partners.</cite> <cite index="46-2,46-4">The company has donated $50,000 to the Thurgood Marshall College Fund and $50,000 to Equal Justice Initiative.</cite>
charity.focus: Food insecurity, racial justice, education, community development

environment.s: <cite index="21-1">Cava is committed to managing energy consumption to help conserve natural resources, enhancing waste management across restaurant operations, and actively measuring and managing carbon footprint.</cite> <cite index="30-19,30-20">The company continued a multi-year project to restore five acres of native Piedmont Savannah habitat around its Verona, Virginia manufacturing facility, and is piloting guest-facing waste stations and exploring eco-friendly packaging solutions including transitioning cutlery to compostable or marine-degradable materials.</cite>

labor.s: <cite index="33-1,33-9">Cava was found noncompliant with all aspects of New York City's Fair Workweek Law, including providing regular schedules, 14-day advance notice, premium pay for schedule changes, and seniority-based reinstatement of laid-off employees.</cite> <cite index="33-11">In 2022, the NYC Department of Consumer and Worker Protection required Cava to pay over $8,000 in restitution to a worker unlawfully terminated in retaliation for taking protected paid time off.</cite> <cite index="24-28,24-29">The company offers benefits including health, dental, vision and mental health insurance, 401K, stock purchase plan, early-wage options, and full-time workers can take up to four paid volunteer days annually.</cite>

dei.s: <cite index="42-2,42-4,42-5">Cava has a 'Diversity Cultivation' competency as key to DEI efforts, offering year-round training on unconscious bias, valuing differences, and inclusive culture.</cite> <cite index="45-9">The company operates an Allies in Motion (AIM) resource group that hosted 38 events in 2024 to increase allyship, empathetic listening, and inclusivity.</cite> <cite index="46-1,46-2">The company has committed to setting diversity goals with annual progress reports and formalizing recruiting programs with Howard University.</cite>

animals.s: As a food service company primarily serving plant-based and Mediterranean cuisine with customizable menu options, animal testing is not applicable to Cava's business model.

guns.s: As a fast-casual restaurant chain focused on Mediterranean food service, Cava does not manufacture, sell, or distribute firearms.

privacy.s: <cite index="78-1,78-4,78-5">Cava does not sell personal information for money but uses tracking technologies that may be considered a 'sale' of personal information under applicable law in California, Colorado, Connecticut, and Texas, and shares personal information for targeted advertising and cross-context behavioral advertising.</cite> The company maintains standard data security practices with SSL encryption and access controls.

execPay.s: <cite index="63-2,63-5">CEO Brett Schulman's total yearly compensation is $1.92M, comprised of 33.8% salary and 66.2% bonuses including company stock and options.</cite> <cite index="63-10">His total compensation of $1.92M is below average for companies of similar size in the US market at $8.22M.</cite> <cite index="64-1,64-5,64-6">Entry-level Team Members earn approximately $32,141 annually while General Managers in Training earn approximately $72,120 based on Glassdoor data.</cite>

sec.note: Pay ratio extracted from proxy filing — verify manually for accuracy
